name: M-theory / string landscape as theory of everything
summary: Classification: TOE-ASPIRATIONAL (canonical top-down). The flagship string-theoretic candidate for a complete theory of physics, composed of: a non-perturbative M-theory definition (BFSS matrix model), a specific compactification (F-theory on an elliptic Calabi-Yau fourfold) embedding SU(5) GUT and Standard-Model matter, KKLT moduli stabilisation giving a discretuum of metastable dS vacua, the string axiverse populating the Kahler-moduli axions, type-I seesaw with thermal leptogenesis for neutrino mass and baryogenesis, axion-monodromy inflation, WIMP dark matter, and anthropic selection across the landscape to fix the cosmological constant and other parameters. Closes the quantum-gravity-consistency puzzle via the string UV completion. The lack of testable predictions and the landscape's vacuum-counting (10^500-10^272000) are the standard criticisms. Coverage of SM-parameter-origin is partial: F-theory geometry constrains Yukawas but does not fix the vacuum.

lineage.citation: Witten, 'String theory dynamics in various dimensions', Nucl. Phys. B 443, 85 (1995)
Banks, Fischler, Shenker, Susskind, 'M theory as a matrix model: A conjecture', PRD 55, 5112 (1997)
Vafa, 'Evidence for F-theory', Nucl. Phys. B 469, 403 (1996)
Kachru, Kallosh, Linde, Trivedi, 'de Sitter vacua in string theory', PRD 68, 046005 (2003)
Susskind, 'The anthropic landscape of string theory' (2003)
Arvanitaki, Dimopoulos, Dubovsky, Kaloper, March-Russell, 'String Axiverse', PRD 81, 123530 (2010)
